---
title: "Predictive Modelling of Coronary Heart Disease in Western Cape Males"
subtitle: "SMM636 Individual Coursework 2025-26"
author: 
  - name: Benjamin Evans
    orcid: 0000-0001-6611-4500
    degrees:
    - PhD
    corresponding: true
    affiliation: City St George's, University of London
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl
categories:
  - MSc
  - SMM636
  - 2025-26
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.model_selection import (
    train_test_split, GridSearchCV, StratifiedKFold
)
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from sklearn.metrics import (
    accuracy_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix,
    roc_auc_score, roc_curve, ConfusionMatrixDisplay
)
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

SEED = 42
np.random.seed(SEED)

# Load data — CSV lives one level up in ../data/
df = pd.read_csv("../data/heart-disease.csv")
```

# Exploratory data analysis {#sec-exploratory-data-analysis}

The dataset comprises 462 adult males from Western
Cape, South Africa, with no missing values across nine predictors and a binary
target (`chd`). The distribution is 302 controls
(65.4%) versus 160 CHD-positive cases (34.6%). 

We know from @zhu2020adjusting,
the effective severity of class imbalance depends jointly on the ratio and the
feature dimensionality. Here, with only $p = 9$ predictors, this imbalance is
relatively minor therefore no required synthetic oversampling...


::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

## Reproducibility

The full analysis code is available as a Jupyter notebook
([`ml-smm636-a02-heart-disease.ipynb`](../ml-smm636-a02-heart-disease.ipynb)) and as a derived Python script
<!-- ([`ml-smm636-a02-heart-disease.py`](../ml-smm636-a02-heart-disease.py)). -->
The dataset is at [`data/heart-disease.csv`](../data/heart-disease.csv).

:::

# References {.unnumbered}
